findings.message: Client now expects 'reserved_count' field in response, but this field is not declared in the committed pact interaction (test_consumer_inventory.py:19). The response body contract only includes 'item_id' and 'in_stock'. This breaks the consumer-provider contract and will cause the pact mock to return an undefined field.
findings.suggestion: Either: (1) Add 'reserved_count' field to the pact interaction in test_consumer_inventory.py and regenerate the pact, or (2) Make the client code handle the optional 'reserved_count' gracefully (e.g., use .get('reserved_count') with a default), or (3) Revert to expecting only 'in_stock' until the provider contract is updated.

findings.message: Return type changed from 'int' to 'dict[str, int]', but the consumer pact test (test_consumer_inventory.py:22) expects the function to return an integer (assert get_stock(...) == 5). This incompatible change will cause the contract test to fail.
findings.suggestion: Update the consumer pact test assertion to expect the new dictionary return type, and ensure the pact interaction includes all fields the client now expects in the response.
